_design_notes.layout: Linear spine (Entrance → West → Center → East → Hub exit) with three branch loops: Generator Tent / Livestock Pavilion off West; Funnel Cake Stand / Prize Ring off Center; Ride Maintenance Bay / Ticket Booth off East. Total 14 rooms.
_design_notes.npc_movement: Hunt target (panicking) starts in Midway Center. Skeptic companion candidate in First Aid Tent. Two bystanders wander: one between West and Food Court, one between East and Ticket Booth.
chain_reactions: CHAIN A — The Generator Fire: deaths_breath enters Generator Tent → precarious_object (heavy wrench) falls → exposed_wiring (generator bus) arcs → gas_source (diesel tank) vents → explosive_ignition. Generator Tent + Midway West.

CHAIN B — The Bull Run: deaths_breath enters Livestock Pavilion → frayed_restraint (pen gate cable) snaps → stampeding_bull charges → food_stall_structure collapses in Food Court → spilled_hot_oil ignites from propane_tanks.

CHAIN C — The Ride Failure: deaths_breath enters Ride Maintenance Bay → precarious_object (loose counterweight) falls → rogue_machinery (ride gondola) activates → structural_stress (ride support tower) cracks → falling_scaffolding.
CHAIN D — The Photo Booth Short: liquid_spill (spilled soda) in Photo Booth reaches exposed_wiring → electrical_surge propagates to Prize Ring midway lights → faulty_generator overloads.
_design_notes.items_seeded: defibrillator_pads in First Aid Tent (finale item). industrial_helium_tank in Balloon Game Stall (finale item). phosphorus_flare in Midway East prize cabinet (recipe component). evidence: fair_program with deaths list context.

Fairgrounds Entrance.name: Fairgrounds Entrance
Fairgrounds Entrance.description: Bright banners and the smell of fried dough. The McKinley 250th Anniversary County Fair sprawls ahead. Ticket stubs litter the ground. The crowd noise is constant, disorienting — the kind of noise that swallows a scream.
Fairgrounds Entrance.first_entry_text: The fair is loud in the way only county fairs are loud — a dozen competing sounds at once, none of them alarming on their own. The smell of diesel and funnel cake. Somewhere ahead, the target is here. So is Death.

objects.name: Fair Program
objects.description: A glossy booklet listing the day's events. Someone has circled the 3PM All-Livestock Showdown in red pen.
objects.examine_details: The circled event is in the Livestock Pavilion at 3PM. The handwriting on the margin reads: 'They'll all be there.'

objects.name: Lost and Found Box
objects.description: A cardboard box of unclaimed items near the gate.
objects.examine_details: A broken watch, a child's shoe, a keyring with a livestock barn tag. Nothing useful.

furniture.name: Gate Booth
furniture.description: The unmanned ticket booth at the entrance.

Ticket Booth.name: Ticket Booth
Ticket Booth.description: A small enclosed booth running ticket sales. Stacks of wristbands on the counter. A single CCTV monitor shows four camera feeds from across the grounds — the only overview you'll get.

objects.name: CCTV Monitor
objects.description: Four grainy feeds. You can see the Livestock Pavilion, the Food Court, the Main Stage, and the Generator Tent. The feed from the Generator Tent is flickering.
objects.examine_details: The Generator Tent feed shows something swaying on a high shelf. The timestamp reads three minutes ago.

furniture.name: Lockbox Under Counter
furniture.description: A small steel lockbox chained to the counter leg.

Midway (West).name: Midway (West)
Midway (West).description: Game stalls line both sides — ring toss, balloon darts, a shooting gallery. Extension cords snake across the ground between rides. The generator hum from the tent to the north is loud enough to feel in your chest.

objects.name: Extension Cord Tangle
objects.description: A rat's nest of orange extension cords running power to six different stalls. One connector is visibly blackened.
objects.examine_details: The blackened connector is hot to the touch. If the generator overloads, this whole tangle becomes a fuse.

objects.name: Shooting Gallery
objects.description: A row of moving metal ducks. The mechanism clicks and whirrs. One of the ducks is stuck, and the motor is grinding.
objects.examine_details: The grinding motor smells hot. You could switch it off — there's a breaker panel on the side of the stall.

furniture.name: Game Stall Counter
furniture.description: A plywood counter loaded with stuffed animal prizes and a cash box.

examine_details.extension cord tangle: Seven different cords, four different gauges. Whoever wired this had no respect for load ratings.
examine_details.shooting gallery: The motor is running hot. A breaker on the side panel could cut power to this whole stall.

Generator Tent.name: Generator Tent
Generator Tent.description: A canvas tent shielding two diesel generators the size of small cars. The smell is exhaust and hot metal. Everything vibrates. A tool bench runs along the back wall — a heavy wrench balanced on the edge, trembling with every pulse of the engine.

objects.name: Tool Bench
objects.description: A scarred workbench. The wrench on the edge is trembling with engine vibration. Someone left it there during a repair and never came back.
objects.examine_details: The wrench is right on the edge. If this tent shakes any harder it will fall onto the bus bar below. You could move it — or leave it.

objects.name: Generator Control Panel
objects.description: Red and green indicators, several already in the red. An emergency cutoff switch is covered by a glass panel.

use.message: You smash the glass and hit the emergency cutoff. Both generators grind down to silence. The chain is broken.

furniture.name: Tool Chest
furniture.description: A red rolling tool chest, partially open.

examine_details.tool bench: The wrench will fall if this room vibrates any harder. Below it: the main bus bar where all the generator output combines.
examine_details.generator control panel: Emergency cutoff under the glass panel. Would kill power to the entire west midway — including the generator chain reaction.

Livestock Pavilion.name: Livestock Pavilion
Livestock Pavilion.description: A long barn smell of hay, manure, and animal heat. Stalls line the walls. The star of the show is Thunder — a 2,300-pound Angus bull in the centre pen, circling his enclosure with his head low. The electronic gate lock is buzzing. Something is wrong with the latch.

objects.name: Thunder's Pen
objects.description: A reinforced steel enclosure. Thunder is circling with his head down. The electronic gate lock is visible on the side — a small LED cycling between green and red.
objects.examine_details: The latch is glitching. Every third cycle it fails to engage. The cable running to the lock is frayed where it passes through the gate hinge.

use.message: You manually secure the pen with the backup padlock. Thunder slams into the gate but it holds.

objects.name: Hay Bales
objects.description: Stacked hay bales line the western wall. Dry as tinder.
objects.examine_details: The hay is bone dry. If the gate electronics throw a spark near here, this whole pavilion goes up.

objects.name: Livestock Ribbon Board
objects.description: Blue and red ribbons line the board. Thunder's name is on three of them.
objects.examine_details: The board shows Thunder won Best in Show three years running. He weighs 2,340 lbs. The note beside his ribbon says: 'Do not approach when agitated.'

furniture.name: Stable Supply Cabinet
furniture.description: A wooden cabinet with veterinary supplies for the livestock.

examine_details.thunder's pen: The electronic latch is failing. One good shock to the system and that gate opens.
examine_details.hay bales: Dry as paper. One spark is all it needs.

Food Court.name: Food Court
Food Court.description: Picnic tables packed with fairgoers. Deep fryers hiss, propane burners roar, and the air is thick with grease. Four food stalls form a square — funnel cake, corn dogs, BBQ, and a fresh-squeezed lemonade stand. The propane array feeding the stalls runs along the back wall on a shared manifold.

objects.name: Propane Manifold
objects.description: Six propane tanks ganged together on a shared shutoff manifold feeding all four stalls. The main shutoff valve is accessible — if someone bothers.

use.message: You wrench the manifold shutoff closed. The propane stoves go out. The chain is broken.

objects.name: Deep Fryer Station
objects.description: Four industrial deep fryers running at 375°F. The nearest one has a crack in the basket hinge — oil is already weeping down the side.
objects.examine_details: Hot oil on a concrete floor is bad. Hot oil on a concrete floor next to propane tanks is a disaster waiting for a spark.

objects.name: Picnic Tables
objects.description: Rows of folding tables full of families eating. They are directly between the propane manifold and the exits.
objects.examine_details: If the manifold goes, everyone at these tables is in the blast radius. They are having a great time. None of them know.

furniture.name: BBQ Stall Counter
furniture.description: A long counter with tongs, squeeze bottles, and a cash box.

examine_details.propane manifold: The shutoff is right there. No special tools required — just the will to do it before someone dies.
examine_details.deep fryer station: One bump and 20 litres of 375-degree oil goes on the floor.

Funnel Cake Stand.name: Funnel Cake Stand
Funnel Cake Stand.description: A standalone stall on the east side of the food court area. The smell is impossibly good. The stall owner has stepped away — a massive vat of oil is left on an open flame, bubbling furiously and getting close to its flash point.

objects.name: Unattended Fryer
objects.description: A commercial fryer left on full heat with nobody watching it. The oil surface is rippling with heat. The thermostat light is off — it stopped regulating.
objects.examine_details: Flash point for canola oil is around 330°C. This looks close. You could shut off the burner from here.

Midway (Center).name: Midway (Center)
Midway (Center).description: The spine of the fair. Rides dominate — a towering Ferris wheel, a scrambler ride, a tilt-a-whirl. Between rides, game stalls. The crowd is thickest here. Somewhere in this crush is the person you're looking for.

objects.name: Ferris Wheel
objects.description: The tallest structure at the fair. The gondolas sway at the top. The operator booth is unattended — the ride is running on automatic.
objects.examine_details: One of the gondola cables is visibly fraying at the top of the wheel. Forty feet up. Nobody at the operator booth.

objects.name: PA Announcement System
objects.description: A standalone PA podium near the center of the midway. Whoever had the mic last left it on.

use.message: Your voice booms across the whole fair. Every head turns toward you. You have about fifteen seconds of attention before people go back to their funnel cake.

furniture.name: Ride Control Kiosk
furniture.description: An unmanned ride control station with joysticks and a red emergency stop.

use.message: You hit the emergency stop. The scrambler winds down. The overhead arms stop swaying.

Prize Ring.name: Prize Ring
Prize Ring.description: A ring-toss stall arcade — six lanes with bottles arrayed on racks. The overhead lights are industrial fluorescents on a shared circuit. One fixture is sparking visibly. The balloon display strung across the top of the stall is directly beneath it.

objects.name: Sparking Light Fixture
objects.description: A fluorescent tube fixture directly above the balloon display. It is actively sparking. This is not ambiguous.
objects.examine_details: Cut the power to this stall or get the balloons away from that fixture. Those are your two options.

objects.name: Helium Manifold
objects.description: A cluster of industrial helium cylinders feeding the balloon inflator. The regulator valve is slightly open — a faint hiss.
objects.examine_details: Industrial helium at pressure. Technically non-flammable, but the manifold is sitting directly under a sparking electrical fixture. The heat alone could rupture the regulator.

use.message: You close the helium manifold regulator. The hissing stops.

furniture.name: Prize Cabinet
furniture.description: A locked glass cabinet behind the counter. Stuffed animals on the shelves and — behind them — a cylindrical case.

Balloon Game Stall.name: Balloon Game Stall
Balloon Game Stall.description: Dart throw lanes. Balloons cover every surface. The helium tanks for inflation stand in a row at the back — eight of them, each taller than you. The stall is unattended. Everything here is pressurised.

objects.name: Helium Tank Array
objects.description: Eight industrial helium cylinders chained to a rack. The chain looks old. One of the tanks has a slow leak — you can feel the faint chill coming from it.
objects.examine_details: If those chains give way and a tank falls, the valve shears off and you have an unguided rocket in a canvas tent.

furniture.name: Dart Lane Counter
furniture.description: A long counter with dart sets and a tip jar. Under the counter, a metal box.

Midway (East).name: Midway (East)
Midway (East).description: The crowd thins out here. Older rides that have seen better decades. A ride maintenance bay sits to the north, its chain-link gate propped open with a fire extinguisher. The exit to the parking lot is east. The rides here are still running.

east.description: The parking lot exit. A chain-link gate. Beyond it, your car.

objects.name: Ferris Wheel Service Scaffold
objects.description: A temporary scaffold erected for a wheel inspection that apparently never finished. It leans against the Ferris wheel support column at an angle that should concern a structural engineer.
objects.examine_details: The scaffold base isn't properly secured. A heavy impact or a sustained vibration could send it into the crowd.

use.message: You secure the scaffold base with the available bolts. It's not perfect, but it won't fall.

objects.name: Ride Junction Box
objects.description: A weathered metal box connecting power to the east-side rides. The lid is corroded open.
objects.examine_details: This box feeds the Ferris wheel, the scrambler, and three game stalls. A surge through this box would hit everything on the east side simultaneously.

furniture.name: Maintenance Equipment Cage
furniture.description: A chain-link cage with ride parts and tools inside. Padlocked but the lock is old.

Ride Maintenance Bay.name: Ride Maintenance Bay
Ride Maintenance Bay.description: A concrete-floored bay behind the east midway. A gondola from a swing ride sits half-disassembled on a hydraulic platform. Spare counterweights are stacked on a shelf above the platform. The hydraulic lift is dripping fluid.

objects.name: Counterweight Shelf
objects.description: A steel shelf bolted above the hydraulic platform. Six concrete counterweights, each 40kg, are stacked in a pyramid. The shelf bracket on the left is cracked.
objects.examine_details: Those counterweights will come off that shelf if the platform vibrates hard enough. They would land directly on the hydraulic platform below.

objects.name: Hydraulic Platform
objects.description: A scissor-lift platform with a half-disassembled gondola on it. The lift is at mid-height, about four feet off the ground, and dripping fluid.
objects.examine_details: The hydraulic fluid on the floor is heading toward the control wiring. If those cables short, the lift will activate on its own.

objects.name: Emergency Pressure Valve
objects.description: A red valve on the hydraulic line. Opening it would bleed the pressure and prevent the lift from activating.

use.message: You open the pressure relief valve. The platform slowly sinks to the floor. The lift can't activate now.

furniture.name: Parts Cabinet
furniture.description: A steel cabinet with ride maintenance parts.

First Aid Tent.name: First Aid Tent
First Aid Tent.description: A proper first aid station — cot, AED, oxygen, bandages. A paramedic sits reading a paperback, radio on their belt. It's quiet in here. The smell of antiseptic. The only quiet room at the fair.

objects.name: AED Unit
objects.description: An Automated External Defibrillator mounted on a pole. Current. Certified. Ready.
objects.examine_details: The AED is functional and stocked. If you needed defibrillator pads, these would do. Permanently attached to the unit though — you'd need to improvise.

use.message: You carefully remove the electrode pads from the AED. The unit will no longer function for its intended purpose. You're hoping this works better.

objects.name: Medical Supply Cabinet
objects.description: A locked cabinet with a red cross sticker. Glass front — you can see bandages, splints, and a small oxygen cylinder inside.

use.message: You break the glass panel. 'In case of emergency' — this qualifies.

examine_details.aed unit: Full kit. The pads are accessible if you're willing to leave this tent without a working defibrillator.
examine_details.medical supply cabinet: Gauze, splints, an epinephrine auto-injector. Worth having.
